The Grand Budapest Hotel (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $59,076,019Details
International Box Office $103,961,642
Worldwide Box Office $163,037,661
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $4,998,812
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $6,751,413
Total $11,750,225
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The adventures of Gustave H., a legendary concierge at a famous European hotel between the wars, and Zero Moustafa, the lobby boy who becomes his most trusted friend. The story involves the theft and recovery of a priceless Renaissance painting and the battle for an enormous family fortune—all against the back-drop of a suddenly and dramatically changing Continent.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$811,166 (1.4% of total gross)
Legs:6.92 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:36.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$31,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/1,467 max. theaters, 7.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $86,800,585

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 7th, 2014 (Limited) by Fox Searchlight
International Releases: March 5th, 2014 (Wide) (Belgium)
March 6th, 2014 (Wide) (Germany)
March 7th, 2014 (Wide) (Austria)
March 7th, 2014 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
March 13th, 2014 (Wide) (Netherlands)
Video Release: June 17th, 2014 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for language, some sexual content and violence.
(Rating bulletin 2299, 11/20/2013)
Running Time: 100 minutes
Award nomination: 2015 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Musical or Comedy, Writers Guild of America Best Original Screenplay Winner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animated Sequences, Breaking the Fourth Wall, Ensemble, Gratuitous Cameos, Intertitle, Old Age Makeup, Prologue, Same Role, Multiple Actors, Unnamed Character, Voiceover/Narration
Plot point: Bigotry, Death of a Son or Daughter, Death of a Spouse or Fiancée / Fiancé, Hitmen, Immigration, Inheritance, May / December Romance, Nazis Outside of World War II, Non-Chronological, Orphan, Prison Break, Rags to Riches, Romance, Winter Sports, Writing and Writers, Wrongfully Convicted
Social setting: Fascism
Subgenre: Comedy Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Fox Searchlight Pictures, Indian Paintbrush, Studio Babelsberg, American Empirical
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English, German, French

  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

The adventures of Gustave H., a legendary concierge at a famous European hotel between the wars, and Zero Moustafa, the lobby boy who becomes his most trusted friend. The story involves the theft and recovery of a priceless Renaissance painting and the battle for an enormous family fortune—all against the back-drop of a suddenly and dramatically changing Continent.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$811,166 (1.4% of total gross)
Legs:6.92 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:36.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$31,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/1,467 max. theaters, 7.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $86,800,585
